properties.address: 2963 NORTH FULTON DR NE, ATLANTA GA 30305
properties.tree_location: On our side of alley on right side of house
properties.tree_description:
25-30 ft Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Ten “Green Giant” Western Red Cedar trees are lined on our side of a 10’ alley adjoining our property. We are located in a residential/limited commercial area and are using our property as a residence. Directly located behind us are multiple businesses sloping down to our property with completely paved lots. When it rains, all the water from these parking lots flows downhill to our property. There is a stormwater drain inlet in the back right corner of our property. During a hard rain, a high volume of water, combined with debris washing down from the aforementioned parking lots can be too much for the drain to handle at once and the water overflows onto the south side of our property, underneath our fence and is eroding the soil up against our retaining wall. We are concerned about the integrity of the retaining wall, which is currently the only thing protecting our house from flooding. We are proposing an overflow outlet/creekbed in place of the trees so that overflow water can naturally escape to the street, where the stormwater drain empties. In order to do this, we would need to remove the trees currently located where the creek bed could go. We do not see any other alternatives for fixing this stormwater issue. If questions, please send out someone to inspect site. Thank you!

		Parcel Number:17 01540008042 *Block:--Lot:--Subdivision:--

This tree is in direct structural conflict with the home due to its extreme proximity to the foundation. At approximately 3 feet from the structure, the
trunk and structural root system are within the foundation bearing zone. As the tree continues to grow, radial trunk expansion and increasing root
diameter will exert pressure on the foundation wall and footing, leading to cracking, displacement, and long-term structural damage.
